material.mattext: Which choice correctly identifies the four components of blood?
red blood cells, thrombocytes, hemoglobin, erythrocytes
plasma, red blood cells, white blood cells, platelets
DNA, anti-serum A, anti-serum B, anti-serum Rh+
Which of the following areas in Alberta would produce the highest red blood cell count within an individual after several days?

A brief anonymous phone call stating that the murder of a man has been committed leads police officers to an old, abandoned farmhouse several kilometres west of Cremona, Alberta. When police officers enter the farmhouse, they discover several dead chickens and a large amount of blood smeared on the walls of the kitchen. What scientific technique may be used to determine if the blood in the kitchen is from the chickens or from a human?

A brief anonymous phone call stating that the murder of a man has been committed leads police officers to an old, abandoned farmhouse several kilometres west of Cremona, Alberta. When police officers enter the farmhouse, they discover several dead chickens and a large amount of blood smeared on the walls of the kitchen. The red blood cells from the blood found in the kitchen prove to be anucleated, meaning the blood likely came from a
Which choice correctly identifies the three protein markers that exist on human red blood cells?
What blood type is the most helpful in the identification of a suspect because it is rare?
Identifying one type of blood enzyme or protein within a blood evidence sample assists investigators because it
determines the type of crime that occurred
establishes the exact time of the crime
helps to narrow down the list of suspects

Some Steps Involved in the Determination of Blood Type of an Unknown Sample

1. Each sample is gently mixed.
2. One type of anti-serum is added to each sample.
3. An agglutination reaction is observed in two samples.
4. Unknown blood is separated into three separate samples.

The sequence of steps that occur in the determination of the blood type of the unknown sample are
When blood evidence is suspected at a crime scene, what should be done initially?
Verify that the evidence found is blood.
Complete a microscopic analysis of the evidence.
Determine the blood type of the victim.
At a crime scene, the first technique that investigators use to find trace blood evidence is
What specific part of blood does phenolphthalein react with?
When phenolphthalein comes into contact with blood, it
Luminol reagent should be used at a crime scene when
the victim's and suspect's blood types are identical
more than one murder victim is present
both animal and human blood are suspected to be involved
it is suspected that the blood evidence has been cleaned away
When luminol comes into contact with blood, it
What specific part of blood does luminol react with?
Iron pigment in red blood cells
The greater the distance that blood free-falls, the greater the increase in the
size of the circular stain pattern and the amount of cast-off residue
amount of blood released and the length of the blood stain pattern
force of the impact and the surface tension in the blood droplets
number of blood droplets and the decomposition of hemoglobin
The blood spatter pattern shown likely was the result of a
Which of the following crime scene objects could be tested to see if a person is a secretor?
The most visible objects in this micrograph of semen is
The normal components of semen are
Sperm cells are unique in that they are the only human body cells that
The substance in semen that the chemical in the fast blue B test reacts with is
If semen is present in a sample, during the fast blue B test it will
After biological evidence is collected from a sexual assault victim and a crime scene, it is sent
to a local hospital for a medicinal examination
to the court house and put into long term storage
to the medical examiner's office and destroyed
to a forensic laboratory for a DNA analysis
What should occur as soon as possible after a sexual assault has been reported by a victim?
The suspect should complete a lie detector test.
The victim should be given a rape kit examination.
The victim should be permitted to clean herself.
The suspect should be allowed to make a statement.
Due to the relative small number of DNA analysts and the increased volume of evidence sent from police agencies, forensic laboratories in Canada have experienced
a backlog of evidence sent in for DNA analysis
an accumulation in the number of not guilty verdicts given
a decrease in the number of sexual assaults reported
a decrease in the turnaround time for DNA analysis
From the list below, check off which are types of biological evidence from a crime scene that will indicate the blood type of a secretor.
